title: Quiz: Cryostat Design
description: Test your understanding of complete cryostat system design for quantum computing, including integration, scalability, and commissioning

questions.question: What is the primary consideration when mounting a quantum processor chip to the mixing chamber?

options.text: Making the mounting as rigid as possible
Minimizing thermal resistance while maintaining good electrical connections
Maximizing the distance from the mixing chamber

questions.explanation: The chip must be thermally well-connected to the mixing chamber to achieve low operating temperature, but also needs proper electrical connections for control and readout. This involves balancing thermal conductance (requiring large contact area and good thermal interface) with practical constraints of wire bonding and chip access.

questions.question: Why must superconducting magnetic shields be cooled below their critical temperature in a low-field environment?

options.text: To prevent the shield from cracking
Because any magnetic flux present during the transition becomes trapped and frozen in the shield
To make the shield mechanically stronger
To reduce the electrical resistance of the shield

questions.explanation: When a superconductor transitions to the superconducting state in the presence of magnetic field, the flux becomes trapped due to flux pinning. This trapped flux cannot be removed without warming above Tc again. Therefore, shields must be cooled through Tc in a pre-screened low-field environment.

questions.question: For a 100-qubit system with individual XY and Z lines plus multiplexed readout (8 qubits/line), approximately how many total cryogenic lines are needed?
questions.explanation: For 100 qubits: 100 XY drive lines + 100 Z flux lines + ceiling(100/8) = 13 readout lines = 213 total lines. This illustrates why wiring is a major scaling challenge - the line count scales nearly linearly with qubit count.

questions.question: What is the typical residual magnetic field specification at the qubit chip location after shielding?
questions.explanation: Good magnetic shielding achieves residual fields below 1 uT (microtesla). This requires multiple layers of mu-metal and often superconducting shields. Earth's field is about 50 uT, so achieving <1 uT requires attenuation of >50x minimum.

questions.question: A cryostat design should maintain at least 30% cooling margin at each temperature stage to account for unexpected heat loads and aging effects.
questions.explanation: True! Maintaining adequate margin (typically 30% or more) is important because: 1) Actual heat loads often exceed calculations, 2) Components degrade over time, 3) Modifications may add heat load later, and 4) Operating at the edge of capacity provides no safety margin for troubleshooting.

questions.question: Which of the following are valid reasons to consider custom cryostat design instead of purchasing a standard commercial system? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Need for extremely high wiring density (500+ lines)
Desire to reach lower base temperatures than possible with standard systems

questions.explanation: High wiring density (a) may exceed standard port availability, existing infrastructure (b) may require custom form factors, and production scale (d) may justify custom optimization. However, standard commercial systems already achieve excellent base temperatures (c) and custom design is unlikely to improve on well-optimized products.

questions.question: During cryostat commissioning, what is the typical cooldown time from room temperature to base temperature for a modern dry dilution refrigerator?
questions.explanation: Modern dry dilution refrigerators typically require 24-48 hours to cool from room temperature to base temperature. This includes ~12-24 hours for the pulse tube to reach 4K, followed by another 12-24 hours for the dilution unit to reach and stabilize at millikelvin temperatures.
